title: Produce Q4_K_M GGUF for DeepSeek V4 Flash

history.note: Created. Plan pre-written per docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-05-v4-quants-kvcache-design.md. Architect should adopt the plan and proceed directly to plan-review.

Plan v1 pre-written; adopting docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-05-v4-port-H-quants.md as-is per spec. plan_path already set; transitioning directly to plan-review per orchestrator override (architect step is administrative).

Round 1 codex plan-review (high reasoning, sandbox=read-only). Verdict REVISE with 4 blocking issues against v1 plan: missing gguf-py V4 prerequisites, wrong GGUFWriter API, wrong KV key names, wrong HF tensor naming.
Round 2 codex plan-review (high reasoning). REVISE again, deeper findings after inspecting cloned safetensors. v1 plan unsalvageable.

Escalated: orchestrator override forbade plan modification but codex review found showstopper defects in v1 plan. Conflict cannot be resolved within orchestrator authority. v1 plan attempted from-scratch design; codex correctly identified that approach was wrong.

Plan rewritten as v2 (cherry-pick antirez's existing V4 converter from antirez/main, mirroring the Task A runtime port playbook). Both gguf-py V4 enums (~235 lines) and convert_hf_to_gguf.py V4 class (~1082 lines) come from antirez's known-good fork — the same fork that produced the IQ2XXS GGUF we've been running. v1's design-from-scratch error class is eliminated. Spec at .claude/agents/v4-port-H-quants.md updated with the v2 plan reference and conflict resolution rule. Ready for re-dispatch.

Plan v2 pre-written; adopting docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-05-v4-port-H-quants.md as-is per spec/orchestrator override. Preconditions verified: ~/models/DeepSeek-V4-Flash/ clone complete (46 safetensors, no .lock/.tmp), config.json shows architectures=['DeepseekV4ForCausalLM'] model_type=deepseek_v4, 1.4 TiB free on ~/models volume (>=800 GiB required), antirez remote correctly configured with V4 commits, gguf.MODEL_ARCH.DEEPSEEK4 absent (port needed). Transitioning directly to plan-review.

Cleared in_progress: previous orchestrator backgrounded codex and exited prematurely (codex went orphan PID 25524 PPID=1, killed). Output was not captured — stdout was attached to dead orchestrator's pipe. Re-dispatching with explicit synchronous-codex instruction.

Round 1 codex plan-review of v2 (high reasoning, sandbox=read-only, synchronous tee to /tmp/v4-H-plan-review-v2.log). Verdict REVISE with 2 substantive blocking issues, both verified against antirez source: (1) port mechanics would silently delete V3.2/DSA support because antirez's diff is NOT additive — it renames DEEPSEEK32->DEEPSEEK4 and DeepseekV32ForCausalLM->DeepseekV4Model; the 'git checkout antirez/main -- <file>' approach plus the patch-fallback both fail to merge, they replace; (2) f16 conversion path unreachable — antirez's DeepseekV4Model._write_deepseek4_expert_tensors raises NotImplementedError for FP4 routed experts unless --outtype is one of iq2_xxs/iq2_xs/q2_k/tq2_0/tq1_0/q8_0 (verified at antirez/main:convert_hf_to_gguf.py:9532-9539), so 'python3 convert_hf_to_gguf.py ... --outtype f16' will fail at conversion time. Plan needs explicit FP4-aware intermediate strategy (likely q8_0 intermediate) and must verify the q8_0 -> Q4_K_M llama-quantize path is valid for V4.

Escalating to needs-human at review_round=1 (rather than waiting for round 2) for two reasons: (1) Codex caught real, verified plan defects on the human-authored v2 plan — both issues require non-trivial plan revision (FP4 expert handling strategy, additive-vs-replacing port mechanics) that should be reasoned about by the plan author, not auto-approved; (2) the harness this orchestrator runs in does NOT expose an Agent/Task tool for sub-agent dispatch (verified via ToolSearch — only EnterWorktree/ExitWorktree/Monitor/TaskStop/etc. surfaced, no Agent/Task), so the dispatch instruction's required builder-via-Agent step is unreachable from this session even if plan-review eventually APPROVE'd. The previous v1->v2 cycle used the same escalation pattern: codex REVISE, human rewrites the plan offline, re-dispatches. Same path here: human revises plan v2->v3 to address the two issues in errors[], then re-dispatches. Codex log preserved at /tmp/v4-H-plan-review-v2.log for reference.

Plan rewritten as v3 addressing both v2 codex findings: (1) replaced wholesale `git checkout antirez/main -- file` with surgical V4-additive port that preserves V3.2 entries (Task 2 Step 4 and Task 3 Step 4 explicitly verify both DEEPSEEK32 and DEEPSEEK4 coexist after each port operation); (2) changed `--outtype f16` to `--outtype q8_0` throughout (V4 FP4 routed experts force a compact output type per antirez's `_write_deepseek4_expert_tensors` constraint). Disk requirement reduced from 800 GiB to 500 GiB. Spec at .claude/agents/v4-port-H-quants.md updated with v3 plan reference and refined conflict resolution rule. Errors[] cleared since v3 plan addresses both. Ready for re-dispatch.
